quoteFirst: 2015. _fully pryme._ See note to Nonne Prestes Tale, B. 4045. _Prime_ commonly means the period from
quoteLast: _Fully prime_ refers to the end of that period, or 9 a.m.; and even _prime_ alone may be used with the same explicit meaning, as in the Nonne Pres. Ta., B. 4387.

quoteFirst: At 7.55 A.M. we started ahead. At 8.20 A.M. it was found that we could not handle the ship by the stern; stopped and attempted to take the line forward. Before it could be made fast, the ship settled so rapidly that the crew was obliged to abandon her, and we hauled the tow-line on board. At
quoteLast: the bow of the _Californian_ went down, apparently resting on bottom. At 9.04 A.M. the stern disappeared and _Corsair_ proceeded. While waiting we hoisted two of the _Californian’s_ boats on board.
title: The Corsair in the war zone
